title: niphobolus gardneri
intro: Niphobolus gardneri, also known as Guduchi or Giloy, is a traditional Indian plant used in Ayurvedic medicine. It is a climbing shrub with yellow flowers and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The plant contains various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, providing several health benefits like improved immune function, reduced oxidative stress, and enhanced wound healing. Niphobolus gardneri is often prepared in herbal tea, decoction, or powder form, and is used in Ayurvedic medicine for its adaptogenic and anti-aging properties.
